name: West Makian

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of West Makian (west2600).

notes: Skeleton. West Makian (Makian Dalam) is a West Papuan (North Halmahera) non-Austronesian language of the Maluku Islands, Indonesia, written in a Latin orthography of the Indonesian type. The grapheme map covers the five-vowel system and a basic consonant inventory including the digraph ng for the velar nasal and c/j for the palatal affricates. Any phonemic glottal stop, conditioned allophony, stress, and length are left unencoded. This is a skeleton drawn from classificatory and general sources; a dedicated phonology would upgrade it.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: West Makian (west2600)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
